title: ecuadorella stacyi
intro: Ecuadorella stacyi, also known as the Ecuadorian sandalwood or "Palos de Sándalo," is a plant species native to the Andes mountain range in Ecuador. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant's essential oil, extracted through steam distillation, is rich in sesquiterpenes, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering health benefits such as reduced pain and inflammation, as well as improved immune function. Ecuadorella stacyi can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or topical cream, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine.
